jokes.text: ♪ Happy birthday, Mr. President ♪ Mr. President? Should I say 'Larry'?
jokes.setup: Singer performs Marilyn Monroe's famous JFK birthday song
jokes.punchline: Realizes the absurdity of calling Larry Sanders 'Mr. President'
jokes.explanation: Sharp deconstruction of celebrity birthday tribute clichés - the pause and question perfectly capture the absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Unknown singer
dialogue.line: ♪ Happy birthday, Mr. President ♪

dialogue.speaker: Unknown singer
dialogue.line: Mr. President? Should I say 'Larry'?

jokes.text: Has anyone else done this idea yet? Only jojo in the commissary.
jokes.setup: Singer worried about originality of Marilyn Monroe tribute
jokes.punchline: Deflating revelation that commissary worker already did it
jokes.explanation: Perfect industry insider joke - the casual deflation of showbiz pretension by mundane reality

dialogue.speaker: Unknown singer
dialogue.line: Has anyone else done this idea yet?

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Only jojo in the commissary.

jokes.text: [Kiss noises] Easy.
jokes.setup: Overly effusive birthday tribute with physical affection
jokes.punchline: Larry's uncomfortable reaction to excessive praise/kissing
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry Sanders character moment - his discomfort with fake showbiz affection in one word

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Easy.

jokes.text: Uh, the insurance people wanted a visual inventory of the office property.
jokes.setup: Larry suspicious about video camera activity
jokes.punchline: Mundane bureaucratic explanation deflates paranoid suspicions
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection playing on Larry's paranoia vs. boring reality

dialogue.line: Uh, the insurance people wanted a visual inventory of the office property.

jokes.text: Nobody's even thinking about your birthday, Larry.
jokes.setup: Larry repeatedly insists he doesn't want birthday attention
jokes.punchline: Assistant's deadpan lie while clearly planning something
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the inevitable surprise party - the lie is so obvious it's funny

dialogue.line: Nobody's even thinking about your birthday, Larry.

jokes.text: For a 55-year-old guy, you look a little heavy. Ouch.
jokes.setup: Hank trying to give Larry a compliment
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that's actually an insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank Kingsley - well-meaning but completely tactless, perfect character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: For a 55-year-old guy, you look a little heavy.
Ouch.

jokes.text: You sounded almost sincere. Well, you can't fake feelings like that.
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on Hank's delivery
jokes.punchline: Hank's oblivious response about not being able to fake sincerity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic exchange - Hank accidentally reveals his insincerity while claiming he can't fake it

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You sounded almost sincere.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, you can't fake feelings like that.

jokes.text: That wasn't bad. It sucked, man.
jokes.setup: Appears to give mild positive feedback
jokes.punchline: Immediate brutal reversal
jokes.explanation: Classic writers' room brutality - the fake-out compliment structure works well

dialogue.line: That wasn't bad. It sucked, man.

jokes.text: He spent the whole time kissing Joel Grey's ass.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining his history with the new writer Mike
jokes.punchline: Specific, oddly detailed complaint about ass-kissing Joel Grey
jokes.explanation: The specificity of Joel Grey makes this memorable - very specific showbiz complaint that feels authentic.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: He spent the whole time kissing Joel Grey's ass.

jokes.text: What finally convinced him, the quality of our riots?
jokes.setup: Discussion of why writer moved to LA
jokes.punchline: Reference to LA riots as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about LA's reputation - good observational comedy with edge

dialogue.line: What finally convinced him, the quality of our riots?

jokes.text: As usual, I'd like to see more of that humor on the page.
jokes.setup: Writer makes clever joke about LA riots
jokes.punchline: Artie's dry response wanting that wit in the actual writing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie line - acknowledges the joke while criticizing the writer's work, classic producer humor

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: As usual, I'd like to see more of that humor on the page.

jokes.text: A shetland pony?
jokes.setup: Hank asking what gifts others are giving Larry
jokes.punchline: Completely random, absurd suggestion
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random - the specificity of 'shetland pony' makes it funnier than just 'pony'

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: A shetland pony?

jokes.text: It looks like something Sammy Davis Jr. would have worn.
jokes.setup: Hank showing off expensive gold bracelet for Larry
jokes.punchline: Comparison to notoriously flashy entertainer
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference joke - Sammy Davis Jr. was known for flashy jewelry, perfect insult disguised as observation

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It looks like something Sammy Davis Jr. would have worn.

jokes.text: I thought we were going to get him gag gifts this year.
jokes.setup: Others giving expensive, thoughtful gifts
jokes.punchline: Jerry's assumption they'd all give joke gifts
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jerry's embarrassment about his cheap gift - good character moment

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I thought we were going to get him gag gifts this year.

jokes.text: I bought Larry plastic vomit.
jokes.setup: Others discussing expensive, thoughtful gifts
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pathetically inappropriate gift choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - the specificity and inappropriateness make it memorable

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I bought Larry plastic vomit.

jokes.text: I'm pretty sure I parked in Nitro's space. He'll probably just pick the car up and move it.
jokes.setup: New writer worried about parking in wrong spot
jokes.punchline: Reference to American Gladiator's superhuman strength
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about TV show crossover - the visual of Nitro picking up a car is funny

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I'm pretty sure I parked in Nitro's space. He'll probably just pick the car up and move it.

jokes.text: I'm sure one will be opening up very soon.
jokes.setup: New writer asking about parking availability
jokes.punchline: Ominous reference to someone getting fired
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor - the threat is clear but delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: I'm sure one will be opening up very soon.

jokes.text: Larry could hardly even look at me. It wasn't my imagination?
jokes.setup: Phil's paranoid observation about Larry's behavior
jokes.punchline: Jerry confirming the snub, validating paranoia
jokes.explanation: Good workplace paranoia comedy - the validation makes it worse

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Larry could hardly even look at me. It wasn't my imagination?

jokes.text: Louis XIII is much more than cognac... After four sips, he fell asleep at the Christmas party. Exactly. That's what Larry likes to do.
jokes.setup: Artie defending expensive cognac as sophisticated gift
jokes.punchline: Reveals Larry just uses it as a sleep aid
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Artie's logic is perfectly twisted, defending waste as intention

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Louis XIII is much more than cognac... After four sips, he fell asleep at the Christmas party. Exactly. That's what Larry likes to do.

jokes.text: Including a $5.00 rush delivery, it's $32.12... There's been a mistake. I ordered Louis XIII.
jokes.setup: Artie boasting about expensive cognac gift
jokes.punchline: Reality of cheap substitute revealed by price
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of pretension - the specific price makes the cheapness concrete

dialogue.speaker: Delivery guy
dialogue.line: Including a $5.00 rush delivery, it's $32.12...

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: There's been a mistake. I ordered Louis XIII.

jokes.text: It's like hot piss.
jokes.setup: Discussing quality of cheap cognac substitute
jokes.punchline: Artie's disgusted description
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective - Artie's bluntness about quality

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: It's like hot piss.

jokes.text: They gave Julio Gallo a very nice funeral. The only problem was when it was time to close the casket, nobody could find the cork.
jokes.setup: Funeral for wine magnate
jokes.punchline: Wine bottle/casket pun
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but feels forced and disconnected from character/situation

dialogue.line: They gave Julio Gallo a very nice funeral. The only problem was when it was time to close the casket, nobody could find the cork.

jokes.text: You have fucked at least three interns that I know about, one right out there behind the fucking desk.
jokes.setup: Artie listing Jerry's unprofessional behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalating to specific sexual misconduct with location detail
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace comedy - the specific location detail makes it memorably inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You have fucked at least three interns that I know about, one right out there behind the fucking desk.

jokes.text: You have to be a genius to get away with that kind of stuff. You're not getting away with it, jer.
jokes.setup: Explaining standards for bad behavior
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Jerry's lack of talent
jokes.explanation: Devastating character moment - Artie's logic is brutally clear and perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You have to be a genius to get away with that kind of stuff. You're not getting away with it, jer.

jokes.text: Don't sweat it, kiddo. It's a good deal. You're getting fired. They have to pay you for a month.
jokes.setup: Artie trying to console Jerry about being fired
jokes.punchline: Presenting getting fired as a 'good deal' because of severance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - brutal honesty delivered as paternal comfort. The twisted logic is character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't sweat it, kiddo. It's a good deal. You're getting fired. They have to pay you for a month.

jokes.text: I just fell in love with this beauty, so I treated myself because I deserve it.
jokes.setup: Hank wearing the bracelet he claimed was for Larry
jokes.punchline: Transparent rationalization for keeping Larry's gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank selfishness - the fake justification is so transparent it's endearing

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I just fell in love with this beauty, so I treated myself because I deserve it.

jokes.text: The network owns the company that makes those. The cost to them is nothing.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on network's exercise bike gift
jokes.punchline: Revealing the corporate synergy behind the 'generous' gift
jokes.explanation: Good insider humor about corporate gift-giving practices

dialogue.line: The network owns the company that makes those. The cost to them is nothing.

jokes.text: He said, 'Clint, do you know Hank?' And do you know what that simple gesture did? It just made my day.
jokes.setup: Hank telling story about Clint Eastwood snubbing him
jokes.punchline: Reference to Eastwood's famous 'Make My Day' line
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting Hank's gratitude to Eastwood's catchphrase - good character integration

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He said, 'Clint, do you know Hank?' And do you know what that simple gesture did? It just made my day.

jokes.text: Some guy offered to blow me for three bucks. What'd you do? I gave the guy three bucks. He looked like he could use it.
jokes.setup: Jerry tells story about proposition
jokes.punchline: Misdirection - he gave money out of pity, not for service
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - the charitable interpretation is unexpected and funny

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Some guy offered to blow me for three bucks. What'd you do? I gave the guy three bucks. He looked like he could use it.

jokes.text: But what did he do, jerry?
jokes.setup: Jerry's ambiguous story about the proposition
jokes.punchline: Persistent questioning about what actually happened
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to previous joke but doesn't add much

dialogue.line: But what did he do, jerry?

jokes.text: Ok, so I'm a little slow, all right? But what I'm saying is, have you made the video yet?
jokes.setup: Larry struggling to understand Sugar Ray's video timeline
jokes.punchline: Larry's frustrated admission of confusion
jokes.explanation: Good talk show host struggle moment - Larry's professional facade cracking

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Ok, so I'm a little slow, all right? But what I'm saying is, have you made the video yet?

jokes.text: Don't get me angry. [Applause, laughter]
jokes.setup: Larry's confused questioning about Sugar Ray's video
jokes.punchline: Sugar Ray's mock-threatening response gets audience reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple celebrity guest moment that works because of the boxing context and Larry's persistence.

dialogue.speaker: Sugar Ray Leonard
dialogue.line: Don't get me angry.

jokes.text: This conversation is not unlike what it must be to fight you, 'cause you feel like... You're surprising me, and I'm not that-- I'm doing the best I can.
jokes.setup: Larry's confusion with Sugar Ray's responses
jokes.punchline: Metaphor comparing interview confusion to boxing match
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor - Larry connecting his interview confusion to boxing creates perfect analogy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This conversation is not unlike what it must be to fight you, 'cause you feel like... You're surprising me, and I'm not that-- I'm doing the best I can.

jokes.text: Those are sissy shoes.
jokes.setup: Crew member making inappropriate comment during show
jokes.punchline: Unprofessional insult about guest's footwear
jokes.explanation: Simple crew unprofessionalism moment - works for behind-the-scenes realism

dialogue.line: Those are sissy shoes.

jokes.text: Knock that shit off, you guys. Sorry, Artie. Listen, I picked that fucking goddamn tie out myself.
jokes.setup: Artie scolding crew through earpiece
jokes.punchline: Artie's profanity-laden defense of his tie choice
jokes.explanation: Good Artie character moment - his pride in wardrobe choices with typical profanity

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Knock that shit off, you guys.
Sorry, Artie.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Listen, I picked that fucking goddamn tie out myself.

jokes.text: Surprise! Beverly, didn't I ask you-- I know. But I surprised you, didn't I?
jokes.setup: Larry explicitly asked for no birthday surprise
jokes.punchline: Beverly's pride in doing exactly what he didn't want
jokes.explanation: Predictable but satisfying payoff to earlier setup about not wanting a party

dialogue.speaker: Party guests
dialogue.line: Surprise!

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Beverly, didn't I ask you--

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: I know. But I surprised you, didn't I?

jokes.text: This is $1,100 a bottle. $75 a shot. And this is the last bottle in the western U.S. You're goddamn right it is.
jokes.setup: Hank presenting expensive cognac as his gift
jokes.punchline: Artie's bitter confirmation of the high price and rarity
jokes.explanation: Great callback - Artie's bitter confirmation after his own cheap bottle debacle

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: This is $1,100 a bottle. $75 a shot. And this is the last bottle in the western U.S.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're goddamn right it is.

jokes.text: Because I know that you love-- gadgets. Math. I love math. And gadgets. Math and gadgets.
jokes.setup: Darlene giving Larry a calculator as gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward attempt to seem grateful for random gift choice
jokes.explanation: Good awkward gift moment - Larry's forced enthusiasm for 'math' is perfectly uncomfortable

dialogue.speaker: Darlene
dialogue.line: Because I know that you love-- gadgets. Math.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I love math. And gadgets. Math and gadgets.

jokes.text: Oh, I feel like Levar Burton in Roots: The Next Generation.

jokes.explanation: Good confused pop culture reference - mixing LeVar Burton's different roles

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, I feel like Levar Burton in Roots: The Next Generation.

jokes.text: Yeah, Artie and I both pitched in on the cognac. No, uh-- So you both pitched in?

jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion about the false claim
jokes.explanation: Classic Hank trying to take credit - his desperation is palpable

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah, Artie and I both pitched in on the cognac.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: No, uh-- So you both pitched in?

jokes.text: Uh, yeah, Larry, I hope I made it clear that the cognac is from both Arthur and myself. So you both pitched in? No, uh--
jokes.setup: Hank trying to share credit for expensive gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's direct question exposes the lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank moment - trying to take credit backfires immediately

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Uh, yeah, Larry, I hope I made it clear that the cognac is from both Arthur and myself.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: So you both pitched in?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No, uh--

jokes.text: That's my gift to Larry. Hey, thanks. Could you blow out the candles? The cake is heavy.

jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup - Jerry's inappropriate gift in formal party context

dialogue.line: Hey, thanks.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Could you blow out the candles?

dialogue.speaker: Larry

jokes.text: Oh, my god. Who threw up? What is that? That's my gift to Larry.
jokes.setup: Francine arriving and seeing something disgusting
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's Hank's plastic vomit gift
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that pays off the plastic vomit setup. Francine's reaction sells the visual gag.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: Oh, my god. Who threw up? What is that?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That's my gift to Larry.

jokes.text: Well, fuck you, ok? Because a, I know I've been fired. And b, if you think I wanna celebrate that fact with you fucks, you're out of your fucking mind.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizing he's been invited to party after being fired
jokes.punchline: Jerry's explosive, profanity-laden breakdown
jokes.explanation: Powerful breakdown moment - Jerry's logic is sound even as he explodes

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, fuck you, ok? Because a, I know I've been fired. And b, if you think I wanna celebrate that fact with you fucks, you're out of your fucking mind.

jokes.text: Larry, is this your little way of letting me know that I've been fired? Well, fuck you, ok? Because a, I know I've been fired. Artie told me. And b, if you think I wanna celebrate that fact with you fucks, you're out of your fucking mind, ok?
jokes.setup: Jerry realizes the party invitation implications
jokes.punchline: His angry logical breakdown of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good structure - the A/B breakdown gives logic to the outburst

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Larry, is this your little way of letting me know that I've been fired? Well, fuck you, ok? Because a, I know I've been fired. Artie told me. And b, if you think I wanna celebrate that fact with you fucks, you're out of your fucking mind, ok?

jokes.text: This is the best party I've ever had.

jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan comment amid the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders moment - his dry delivery amid chaos is quintessential character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: This is the best party I've ever had.

jokes.text: I remember right before I gave up stand-up, I was at Catch a Rising Star, and I was bombing horribly. And there was one guy in the back that was laughing, and it was you.
jokes.setup: Jerry's video tribute to Larry
jokes.punchline: Touching moment of gratitude from fired writer
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment - Jerry's sincerity after his explosion adds depth

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I remember right before I gave up stand-up, I was at Catch a Rising Star, and I was bombing horribly. And there was one guy in the back that was laughing, and it was you.

jokes.text: Happy birthday, asshole. [Rock thuds]
jokes.setup: Jerry making touching tribute video
jokes.punchline: Jerry throwing rock at building, combining sentiment with violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect end to Jerry's arc - combining genuine affection with justified anger in one gesture

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
